'''Aidan Yüçel Sezer''' (born 24 June 1991) is an [[Australia]]n<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/10/06/turkey-hoping-aidan-sezer-can-provide-finals-boost-at-emerging-nations-world-championship/ |title=Turkey hoping Sezer can provide World Championship boost |last=Walter |first=Brad |date=6 October 2018 |website= |publisher=[[National Rugby League]] (NRL) |access-date= |quote= }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12214/11920889/super-league-2020-huddersfield-giants-aidan-sezer-stays-in-touch-with-turkish-roots |title=Super League 2020: Huddersfield Giants' Aidan Sezer stays in touch with Turkish roots |last=Bazeley |first=Marc |date=1 February 2020 |website= |publisher=[[Sky Sports]] |access-date= |quote= }}</ref> professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} and {{rlp|so}} for [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Gold Coast Titans]], [[Canberra Raiders]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] and  the [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]]. Sezer has played for the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]] and [[City New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales City]] sides.

==Background==
Sezer was born in [[Bankstown]], [[Sydney]], [[New South Wales]], [[Australia]] to a [[Turkish Australians|Turkish]] father, Yüçel, and an [[Indigenous Australian]] mother.<ref>{{cite web|last=Meyn|first=Travis|url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/aidan-sezer-has-shone-for-the-titans-now-he-is-set-to-become-a-star/story-fndv2zxt-1226555462796|title=Aidan Sezer has shone for the Titans, now he is set to become a Star|website=News.com.au|date=2013-01-17|access-date=2016-01-31|archive-date=19 January 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130119161905/http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/aidan-sezer-has-shone-for-the-titans-now-he-is-set-to-become-a-star/story-fndv2zxt-1226555462796|url-status=live}}</ref>

Sezer played his junior football for the [[East Hills Bulldogs]] before being signed by the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]]. Sezer played in the Bulldogs [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|Toyota Cup]] team in 2010–2011, scoring over 300 points.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/player/s/sezer_aidan/index.php|title=Titans trio head west in name of Indigenous education|access-date=17 April 2012|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120512021635/http://rleague.com/db/player/s/sezer_aidan/index.php|archive-date=12 May 2012 }}</ref> On 31 August 2010, Sezer was named at [[Rugby league positions#Five-eighth|five-eighth]] in the 2010 [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] Team of the Year.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/toyota-cup-team-of-the-year-revealed/tabid/10871/newsid/60002/default.aspx|title=Toyota Cup Team of the Year revealed|website=NRL.com|access-date=2016-01-31|archive-date=3 February 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160203095838/http://www.nrl.com/toyota-cup-team-of-the-year-revealed/tabid/10871/newsid/60002/default.aspx|url-status=live}}</ref> At the end of 2011, Sezer signed with the [[Gold Coast Titans]] after a lack of first-grade opportunities at the Bulldogs.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/gold-coast-titans-slam-todd-carney-rumours-3678/|title=Gold Coast Titans slam Todd Carney rumours | NRL|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2011-08-23|access-date=2016-01-31|archive-date=3 February 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160203095837/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/gold-coast-titans-slam-todd-carney-rumours-3678/|url-status=live}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===2012===
In round 7 of the [[2012 NRL season]], Sezer made his NRL debut for the [[Gold Coast Titans]] against the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] at [[Rugby league positions#Five-eighth|five-eighth]] and scored a try in the Titans 26–14 upset win at [[Brookvale Oval]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.qt.com.au/story/2012/04/11/sezer-makes-nrl-debut/|title=Sezer makes NRL debut | Queensland Times|website=Qt.com.au|date=2012-04-11|access-date=2016-01-31|archive-date=15 March 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210315110912/https://www.qt.com.au/news/sezer-makes-nrl-debut/1337050/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/manly-sea-eagles-host-gold-coast-titans-in-round-seven-nrl-clash-at-brookvale-oval/story-fn2mcuj6-1226326661154|title=Home | Live Scores & Latest News|work=Fox Sports|access-date=2016-01-31}}</ref><ref name="heraldsun1">{{cite web|url=http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/nrl/titans-five-eighth-aidan-sezer-signs-new-deal-until-2015/story-e6frfgbo-1226529562655|title=Titans Five Eighth Aidan Sezer Signs New Deal Until 2015|work=Herald Sun|access-date=2016-01-31}}</ref> Sezer finished his debut year in the NRL with him playing in 18 matches, scoring four tries, kicking seven goals and kicking one field goal. On 4 December, Sezer re-signed with the Gold Coast for three years until the end of the [[2015 NRL season]].<ref name="heraldsun1"/>

===2013===
On 9 February 2013, Sezer played for the [[Indigenous All-Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]] team in the [[2013 All Stars match]] against the [[NRL All Stars team|NRL All Stars]] off the interchange bench in the Indigenous Team 32–6 win at [[Suncorp Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/2013-all-stars-teams-announced/tabid/10874/newsid/70589/default.aspx|title=2013 All Stars teams announced|website=NRL.com|date=16 January 2013 |access-date=2016-01-31|archive-date=19 January 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130119142754/http://www.nrl.com/2013-all-stars-teams-announced/tabid/10874/newsid/70589/default.aspx|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|last=Burke|first=Dominic|url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/live-indigenous-all-stars-v-nrl-all-stars-at-suncorp-stadium/story-fndujljl-1226574515914|title=REPLAY: Indigenous All Stars 32 d NRL All Stars 6 at Suncorp Stadium|website=News.com.au|date=2013-02-09|access-date=2016-01-31|archive-date=13 August 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150813134743/http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/live-indigenous-all-stars-v-nrl-all-stars-at-suncorp-stadium/story-fndujljl-1226574515914|url-status=live}}</ref> Sezer finished the [[2013 NRL season]] with him playing in 22 matches, scoring one try, kicking 75 goals and kicking two field goals for the [[Gold Coast Titans]].

===2014===
In round 10 against the [[Brisbane Broncos]] at [[Suncorp Stadium]], Sezer suffered a pectoral muscle injury in the Gold Coast's 22–8 loss which made him miss three months of football,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/the-brisbane-broncos-have-beaten-the-gold-coast-titans-228-at-suncorp-stadium/story-e6frf3xc-1226920782013|title=Home | Live Scores & Latest News|work=Fox Sports|access-date=2016-01-31|archive-date=11 June 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140611183246/http://www.foxsports.com.au//nrl/nrl-premiership/the-brisbane-broncos-have-beaten-the-gold-coast-titans-228-at-suncorp-stadium/story-e6frf3xc-1226920782013|url-status=live}}</ref> until making a return in Round 22 against the [[Sydney Roosters]] at [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|SFS]] which the Gold Coast lost the match 26–18.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/sydney-roosters-boost-their-hopes-of-a-top-four-finish-with-a-2618-victory-over-a-battling-gold-coast/story-fn2mcuj6-1227020840035|title=Home | Live Scores & Latest News|work=Fox Sports|access-date=2016-01-31|archive-date=11 September 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140911121049/http://www.foxsports.com.au//nrl/nrl-premiership/sydney-roosters-boost-their-hopes-of-a-top-four-finish-with-a-2618-victory-over-a-battling-gold-coast/story-fn2mcuj6-1227020840035|url-status=live}}</ref> Sezer finished the Gold Coast Titans [[2014 NRL season]] with him playing in 13 matches, scoring four matches, kicking 23 goals and kicking one field goal in an injury riddled year.

===2015===
On 20 January, Sezer was named in the Gold Coast [[2015 NRL Auckland Nines]] squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/titans-2015-nines-squad/|title=TITANS NAME 2015 NINES SQUAD|work=Rugby League Week|date=2015-01-20|access-date=2016-01-31|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160124040230/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/titans-2015-nines-squad/|archive-date=24 January 2016|url-status=dead}}</ref> On 10 April, he signed a three-year contract with the [[Canberra Raiders]] starting in 2016.<ref>{{cite web|author=Chris Wilson|url=http://www.canberratimes.com.au/rugby-league/canberra-raiders/canberra-raiders-sign-aidan-sezer-20150409-1mhv27.html|title=Canberra Raiders sign Aidan Sezer|website=The Canberra Times|date=9 April 2015 |access-date=2016-01-31|archive-date=24 September 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150924034349/http://www.canberratimes.com.au/rugby-league/canberra-raiders/canberra-raiders-sign-aidan-sezer-20150409-1mhv27.html|url-status=live}}</ref> He finished his last year with the Gold Coast, having played in 18 matches, scoring three tries, kicking 51 goals and one field goal.

===2016===
In round 1 of the [[2016 NRL season]], Sezer made his club debut for the [[Canberra Raiders]] against the [[Penrith Panthers]], playing at halfback and scoring two tries in the club's 30–22 win at [[Canberra Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/raiders/canberra-raiders-down-penrith-panthers-but-lose-star-blake-austin-to-injury/news-story/790577d04a1c8d1641d8cc219e6fdf84|title=Canberra Raiders Down Penrith Panthers but Lose Star Blake Austin to Injury|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Australia|access-date=2016-03-06|archive-date=3 April 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160403051331/http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/raiders/canberra-raiders-down-penrith-panthers-but-lose-star-blake-austin-to-injury/news-story/790577d04a1c8d1641d8cc219e6fdf84|url-status=live}}</ref> On 8 May 2016, Sezer played for [[City New South Wales rugby league team|City Origin]] against [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|Country Origin]], where he played at five-eighth and kicked 6 goals in the 44–30 win in [[Tamworth, New South Wales|Tamworth]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/young-city-side-stun-country/tabid/10874/newsid/95694/default.aspx|title=Young City side stun Country|date=8 May 2016 |publisher=NRL.com|access-date=2016-05-12|archive-date=26 June 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180626163701/https://www.nrl.com/news/2016/05/08/young-city-side-stun-country/|url-status=live}}</ref> Sezer finished the [[2016 NRL season]] with him playing in 24 matches, scoring 5 tries and kicking 5 goals in his first year with the Canberra club.

===2017===
In February 2017, Sezer was selected in Canberra's 2017 Auckland Nines squad.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/02/04/2017-downer-nrl-auckland-nines-squads/ |title=2017 Downer NRL Auckland Nines squads - NRL |date=3 February 2017 |access-date=6 January 2018 |archive-date=30 December 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171230114613/https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/02/04/2017-downer-nrl-auckland-nines-squads/ |url-status=live }}</ref> On 10 February 2017, Sezer played for the Indigenous All Stars against the [[World All Stars]] in the [[2017 All Stars match]], playing off the interchange bench in the 34–8 win at [[Hunter Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/indigenous-all-stars-thump-world-all-stars-348-in-newcastle/news-story/a016f102b1c7ddc5bbdeeedb83a149c5 |title=Indigenous All Stars thump World All Stars 34-8 in Newcastle | Daily Telegraph |access-date=6 January 2018 |archive-date=13 February 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170213044809/http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/indigenous-all-stars-thump-world-all-stars-348-in-newcastle/news-story/a016f102b1c7ddc5bbdeeedb83a149c5 |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/canberra-raiders/canberra-raiders-halfback-aidan-sezer-uses-nrl-all-stars-to-learn-from-north-queensland-cowboys-johnathan-thurston-20170227-gumack.html?deviceType=text |title=Canberra Raiders halfback Aidan Sezer uses NRL All Stars to learn from North Queensland Cowboys' Johnathan Thurston |date=27 February 2017 |access-date=6 January 2018 |archive-date=6 January 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180106121806/http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/canberra-raiders/canberra-raiders-halfback-aidan-sezer-uses-nrl-all-stars-to-learn-from-north-queensland-cowboys-johnathan-thurston-20170227-gumack.html?deviceType=text |url-status=live }}</ref> In round 5 against the [[Parramatta Eels]], Sezer played his 100th NRL career match in the 30–18 win at [[Canberra Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.canberratimes.com.au/rugby-league/canberra-raiders/aidan-sezer-wants-to-take-control-of-the-canberra-raiders-ahead-of-his-100th-nrl-game-20170326-gv7337.html |title=Aidan Sezer wants to take control of the Canberra Raiders ahead of his 100th NRL game |date=27 March 2017 |access-date=6 January 2018 |archive-date=6 January 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180106133344/http://www.canberratimes.com.au/rugby-league/canberra-raiders/aidan-sezer-wants-to-take-control-of-the-canberra-raiders-ahead-of-his-100th-nrl-game-20170326-gv7337.html |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/04/01/cotric-stunner-seals-raiders-victory/ |title=Cotric stunner seals Raiders victory - NRL |date=April 2017 |access-date=6 January 2018 |archive-date=6 January 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180106173040/https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/04/01/cotric-stunner-seals-raiders-victory/ |url-status=live }}</ref> Sezer finished the [[2017 NRL season]] with him playing in all of the Raiders 24 matches, scoring 4 tries and kicking 12 goals. Sezer and his halves partner [[Blake Austin]] were subjected to criticism at the end of the season for not lifting their game to close out matches and were taking the back seat for hooker [[Josh Hodgson]] who did the key playmaking a little too often than they should have.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/raiders/5-reasons-to-be-excited-about-the-raiders-in-nrl-season-2018/news-story/769f47cdfbb7118968a008472f188007 |title=No Hodgson but Raiders are still title contenders  |access-date=6 January 2018 |date= 2018}}</ref>

===2018===
On 27 June 2018, Sezer extended his contract with the Canberra club to the end of the [[2020 NRL season]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/canberra-raiders-re-sign-aidan-sezer-20180627-p4zo0k.html |title=Canberra Raiders re-sign Aidan Sezer |date=27 June 2018 |access-date=31 March 2019 |archive-date=31 March 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190331095711/https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/canberra-raiders-re-sign-aidan-sezer-20180627-p4zo0k.html |url-status=live }}</ref> Sezer finished the [[2018 NRL season]] with 18 matches, 2 tries, 8 goals and 1 field goal. In October 2018, Sezer alongside Raiders team-mate [[Emre Guler]] trained with the [[Turkey national rugby league team]] for the [[2018 Emerging Nations World Championship]] matches.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/10/06/turkey-hoping-aidan-sezer-can-provide-finals-boost-at-emerging-nations-world-championship/ |title=Turkey hoping Aidan Sezer can provide finals boost at Emerging Nations World Championship - NRL |date=5 October 2018 |access-date=31 March 2019 |archive-date=31 March 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190331095715/https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/10/06/turkey-hoping-aidan-sezer-can-provide-finals-boost-at-emerging-nations-world-championship/ |url-status=live }}</ref>

===2019===
Sezer started the [[2019 NRL season]] as the club's first choice halfback and kicked a field goal in the opening round as Canberra defeated the Gold Coast 21–0.  In round 2, Sezer suffered an oblique injury and was replaced by [[Sam Williams (rugby league)|Sam Williams]].  Due to the good form of Canberra and Williams, Sezer was kept in reserve grade to play for Canberra's feeder club side the [[Mount Pritchard Mounties]] after recovering from his injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/05/02/sezer-willing-to-wait-in-the-wings/|title=Sezer willing to wait in the wings|website=nrl.com|date=May 2019 |access-date=13 May 2019|archive-date=13 May 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190513150551/https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/05/02/sezer-willing-to-wait-in-the-wings/|url-status=live}}</ref>

In round 23, Sezer played his 150th NRL game in Canberra's 14–18 loss to the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] at [[Canberra Stadium|GIO stadium]] in Canberra. In round 24, Sezer kicked 3 field goals in Canberra's 15–14 win against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] at [[Shark Park|Pointsbet Stadium]], including the game winner in golden point extra time.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2019-09-01/nrl-scorecentre-sharks-raiders-dragons-wests-tigers/11462176/|title=Canberra Raiders ruin Paul Gallen's Cronulla farewell, Tigers beat Dragons to stay in finals hunt|newspaper=ABC News|date=September 2019 |access-date=2 September 2019|archive-date=2 September 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190902054535/https://www.abc.net.au/news/2019-09-01/nrl-scorecentre-sharks-raiders-dragons-wests-tigers/11462176|url-status=live}}</ref>

Sezer played for Canberra in the [[2019 NRL Grand Final]] which was their first decider in 25 years. Canberra would go on to lose the final 14–8 against the [[Sydney Roosters]] at [[ANZ Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-grand-final-player-ratings-roosters-and-raiders-hits-and-misses-20191006-p52y53.html/|title=NRL grand final player ratings: Roosters and Raiders hits and misses|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=6 October 2019 |access-date=7 October 2019|archive-date=7 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191007084154/https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-grand-final-player-ratings-roosters-and-raiders-hits-and-misses-20191006-p52y53.html|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49950757|title=Sydney Roosters beat Canberra Raiders to win NRL Grand Final|work=BBC Sport |access-date=7 October 2019|archive-date=7 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191007075920/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49950757|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/grand-final-ricky-stuart-reaction-canberra-raiders-six-again-jack-wighton/ae39b639-d375-4608-89a6-af6285c3d635/|title=How Ricky Stuart reacted to grand final 'six-again' refereeing shocker|publisher=WWOS|access-date=7 October 2019|archive-date=7 October 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191007092607/https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/grand-final-ricky-stuart-reaction-canberra-raiders-six-again-jack-wighton/ae39b639-d375-4608-89a6-af6285c3d635|url-status=live}}</ref>

On 28 November, it was announced that Sezer had signed a two-year deal to join [[Super League]] side [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-sign-nrl-star-17327239/|title=Huddersfield Giants sign NRL star on two-year deal|website=Examiner Live|access-date=2 December 2019|archive-date=13 June 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210613094634/https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-sign-nrl-star-17327239|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===2020===
Sezer made 16 appearances for Huddersfield in the [[2020 Super League season]] as the club missed out on the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/1800/season-review-huddersfield-giants/|title=Huddersfield Giants season review|website=www.superleague.co.uk}}</ref>

===2021===
In round 5 of the [[2021 Super League season]], Sezer scored two tries and kicked two goals as Huddersfield defeated [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] 14–13 to claim their first win of the year.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12293586/ian-watson-huddersfield-giants-head-coach-feeling-good-after-first-super-league-win-of-2021/|title=Huddersfield Giants head coach feeling good after first Super League win of 2021|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

On [[International rugby league in 2021#June|25 June 2021]] he played for the [[Combined Nationalities rugby league team|Combined Nations All Stars]] in their 26–24 victory over [[England national rugby league team|England]], staged at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]], [[Warrington]], as part of England's [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] preparation.

On 19 July, it was announced that Sezer would leave [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] at the end of 2021 to take up a two-year deal with eight time Super League Champions [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Sky Sports|date=19 Jul 2021|accessdate=31 Dec 2021|title=Aidan Sezer: Leeds Rhinos sign Huddersfield Giants half-back for 2022 Super League season|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12358869/aidan-sezer-leeds-rhinos-sign-huddersfield-giants-half-back-for-2022-super-league-season}}</ref>

===2022===
[[File:SeyfarthansSezar.jpg |thumb|right|Sezer (left) with teammate [[Alex Seyfarth]]|300px]]In round 1 of the 2022 Super League season, Sezer made his club debut for Leeds in their 22–20 loss against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.therhinos.co.uk/report/leeds-rhinos-20-warrington-wolves-22/|title=LEEDS RHINOS 20 WARRINGTON WOLVES 22|website=www.therhinos.co.uk|date=12 February 2022 }}</ref>
Sezer missed the clubs 2022 Super League Grand Final loss against [[St Helens R.F.C.]] due to failing concussion protocols.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/leeds-rhinos-aidan-sezer-speaks-on-missing-grand-final-as-he-praises-atmosphere-in-english-game-60027/|title=Leeds Rhinos' Aidan Sezer speaks on missing Grand Final as he praises atmosphere in English game|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com}}</ref>

===2023===
On 21 August, Sezer signed a one-year deal to join the [[Wests Tigers]] ahead of the 2024 NRL season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2023/08/21/sezer-returns-to-nrl-with-wests-tigers/|title=Sezer returns to NRL with Wests Tigers|website=www.weststigers.com.au}}</ref>
Sezer played a total of 19 games for Leeds in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished 8th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-2023-season-review-picking-out-the-best-and-worst-players-performances-results-4355920/|title=Leeds Rhinos' 2023 season review: picking out the best and worst players, performances, results|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref>

===2024===
In round 2, Sezer made his club debut for the Wests Tigers in their loss against Canberra. In round 4, he kicked the winning field goal for the Wests Tigers as they upset Parramatta 17-16.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2024-04-01/nrl-monday-blog-parramatta-eels-wests-tigers/103654780/|title=Clint Gutherson misses post-siren penalty after Aidan Sezer's game-winning field goal in last minute of Tigers-Eels clash|website=www.abc.net.au}}</ref> In round 9, Sezer was placed on report but not sin-binned for a supposed hip-drop on [[Joshua Curran]] in the Tigers' 22-14 loss against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-05-04 |title=Crichton, Kikau lead Bulldogs to comeback victory |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2024/05/04/bulldogs-climb-into-top-eight-with-comeback-victory/ |access-date=2024-06-12 |website=National Rugby League |language=en}}</ref> NRL head of football operations [[Graham Annesley]] would later state that Sezer's tackle was not a hip-drop, while also stating that Sezer should have been binned for dangerous contact instead.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-05-06 |title=‘They cleared it’: Why Sezer’s tackle isn’t a hip drop... but he should still have been binned |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2024-nrl-head-of-football-graham-annesley-weekly-football-briefing-wests-tigers-halfback-aidan-sezer-hip-drop-tackle-suspension-news-videos/news-story/9c30fa7fb64b1f1828bc849e591bfa2e |access-date=2024-06-12 |website=Fox Sports |language=en}}</ref> Sezer was later suspended for 4 weeks after the incident.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-05-05 |title=‘Really ugly’: Tigers halfback cops HUGE ban for hip drop tackle |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2024-aidan-sezer-hip-drop-no-sin-bin-confusion-bulldogs-vs-tigers-kasey-badger-reaction-rugby-league-news/news-story/448be26d1646dad11485a7d94b6c6fb9 |access-date=2024-06-12 |website=Fox Sports |language=en}}</ref>
Sezer played 17 games for the Wests Tigers throughout the [[2024 NRL season]] as the club finished with the [[Wooden Spoon (award)|wooden spoon]] for a third consecutive year.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2024-wests-tigers-v-parramatta-eels-spoon-bowl-lachlan-galvin-reaction-social-media-rugby-league-news/news-story/52d07b10167bea71afbbb846921d969c/|title=‘Either the best or worst game all year’: NRL world ‘can’t look away’ from Spoon Bowl|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

On 12 September 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Hull F.C.]] in the Super League on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=12 Sep 2024|accessdate=3 Oct 2024|title=Hull FC bring in half-back Sezer|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c20l0rvm2pvo}}</ref>

===2025===
In round 1 of the 2025 Super League season, Sezer made his club debut for Hull F.C. in their upset victory over [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c20p9z9lvg7o|title=Two tries for Cust as new-look Hull FC beat Catalans|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Sezer played 25 games for Hull F.C. in the 2025 Super League season as the club finished 7th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/grading-each-of-super-leagues-bottom-six-in-2025-with-warrington-getting-the-lowest-marks/|title=Grading each of Super League’s bottom six in 2025 with Warrington getting the lowest marks|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>
